danger_signals.visual_form: UFO icons appearing on global map display
danger_signals.meaning: Active alien threat requiring interception or ground response

danger_signals.visual_form: Alien base markers on Geoscape map
danger_signals.meaning: Entrenched enemy presence requiring strategic assault
Alien aggression against civilian populations; loss of funding if unresponded
Political instability; nations signing pacts with aliens or reducing support
Cascading threat—two consecutive poor months triggers game-over scenario

safety_signals.meaning: Established foothold for defense, research, and personnel staging

safety_signals.visual_form: Aircraft markers on Geoscape in patrol or intercept status
safety_signals.meaning: Active defensive coverage and UFO response capability

safety_signals.visual_form: UFO downed on map; transition to Battlescape tactical mission
safety_signals.meaning: Enemy craft neutralized; opportunity to salvage alien technology and research

safety_signals.visual_form: Geoscape funding bar or nation roster showing continued backing
safety_signals.meaning: Political stability and continued resource flow for X-COM operations

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: X-COM bases displayed on Geoscape; X-COM aircraft icons with distinct visual identification

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple alien species controlled by Ethereals (enslaved races); mutated alien creatures in Battlescape
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: UFO craft icons on Geoscape; alien base markers; terror attack zones; crashed/landed UFO sites
Nation status indicators on Geoscape interface; funding bar reflecting collective support; potential defection indicators

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Early game (limited alien activity, conventional weapons only)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Few UFO sightings on Geoscape; limited alien base markers; early research tree available
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Aliens conduct reconnaissance missions to gather Earth intelligence; sporadic abductions and attacks

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Mid game (escalating alien presence, hybrid tech available)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased UFO density on Geoscape; multiple alien bases established; mixed conventional and alien equipment in arsenal
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Aliens intensify terror attacks on cities; conduct direct assaults on X-COM bases; research operations accelerate

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Late game (advanced alien technology, Mars base revealed)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Heavy UFO activity; Cydonia region revealed on Geoscape; high-tier alien equipment available for research
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Aliens consolidate power; Ethereal presence confirmed through captured specimens; final assault on Cydonia becomes critical path

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Funding nations removed from roster or marked as compromised; funding bar declining to critical levels; Geoscape shows nation-specific icons greyed out
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Funding withdrawal accelerates; alien pacts formed with defected nations; X-COM operations severely constrained

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alien Brain biocomputer destroyed at Cydonia; UFO activity ceases on Geoscape; alien base markers disappear
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Alien threat neutralized; remaining alien forces collapse; humanity secures alien technology

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All X-COM bases captured (markers disappear from Geoscape); funding nations defect en masse; Cydonia assault results in total loss
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Aliens establish planetary dominion; terraforming operations commence; remaining humans enslaved or eliminated

environmental_storytelling.example: Downed alien craft locations on Geoscape serve as mission sites; wreckage examination and artifact collection communicate alien technology progression and military capacity
Multiple alien base icons appearing on Geoscape over time visually demonstrates escalating alien colonization and strategic control of Earth regions
Clustered attack markers on specific continental regions show alien targeting priorities and psychological warfare strategy to destabilize funding nation governments
Visual list of 16 founding nations with funding contribution bars communicates geopolitical fragility; defections show world governments abandoning X-COM under alien pressure
Unlocking alien technology in research interface shows humanity's learning curve and technological gap closure; visual tree structure communicates interdependencies
Alien ship interiors, desert UFO crash sites, and urban terror sites provide environmental storytelling of alien operational diversity and planetary infiltration tactics

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moving across Geoscape toward cities, X-COM bases, or UFO interception points; appears as moving icon on map
Stationary on Geoscape; triggers mission availability for ground team deployment; visual marker indicates salvage opportunity
Continuously spawns UFO missions; marker remains on Geoscape; represents persistent alien infrastructure
Circular movement pattern on Geoscape; defensive posture; ready to intercept
Direct vector toward detected UFO; engagement occurs off-screen; combat result displayed as UFO destroyed/intercepted or escaped
Maintains funding contribution; remains on nation roster; provides monthly resource allocation
Experiences terror attacks; funding bar decreases; visual indicator shows pressure from alien activity
Removed from roster; funding ceases; signs pact with aliens (communicated through plot event); strategic response unavailable in that region

notes: X-COM: UFO Defense uses a dual-interface visual communication system: (1) Geoscape provides strategic-scale world-state visualization through icon-based global mapping showing faction presence, threats, and political stability; (2) Battlescape shifts to tactical detail view for ground-level engagement. The game communicates danger through proliferating threat markers (UFOs, alien bases, terror attacks) and political instability through funding nation status degradation. Faction identity is signaled through mission context and unit type rather than explicit color-coding. World-state progression is communicated through research tree unlocks, available technology, and escalating alien activity density on the Geoscape. Defeat condition visualization involves cascading system failures: base losses, nation defections, and funding depletion—all displayed through interface elements disappearing or greying out.
